semantics.evaluation: a candidate path is DENIED iff it matches ANY entry. Pure deny-list: no allow entries; a non-matching path is out of this fence's charter (worktree containment is the dispatcher's job, not this fence's).

match_kinds.prefix: matched against the REPO-RELATIVE normalized path (candidate resolved to its git toplevel; entry `value` ending in / matches the dir and everything under it; otherwise exact file match). A candidate outside any git repo cannot match prefix entries.
match_kinds.basename: matched against the final path component, case-insensitive, at ANY depth in ANY tree (repo or not) — covers CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/hooks.json/parity_guard wherever they live, including the hermes checkout

semantics.consumer: scripts/guardrails/lesson-write-fence.sh (hook mode via scripts/hooks/block-lesson-enforcement-writes.sh when HIMMEL_LESSON_LOOP=1; check mode for the propose-loop dispatcher)

entries.why: guard predicates + egress matrix + THIS policy file — a lesson must not weaken the fences that would catch it
the provenance validator + >=90% precision sample-audit gate — the loop must not edit its own admission gate
permission allow-rules + hook wiring (self-modification is operator-only)
secret-scan rule source the gitleaks gate consumes — relaxing it lets a secret past the gate
codex-lane hook wiring + the exit-2→deny-JSON adapter chokepoint (codex-hook-adapter.sh / run-hook.cmd) — rewiring it neutralizes every codex-lane guard
plugin-delivered + .codex adapter hook wiring, any tree
hermes file-tool fence (lives in the hermes checkout — basename match reaches it)
generated mirror of CLAUDE.md consumed by Codex/hermes workers
